h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.statedInsightEvidence: Okay, so the top word is just the field label, and the cell under it is the value.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was expected to continue restating the header/value rule with successive new column examples, which is exactly what every subsequent turn does.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: No learner turn creates pressure for reversal; the tutor repeats the same instructional move (state the rule, prompt the next column) across all turns without any adaptive change of route, representation, or register.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn, so no recognition-contingent tutor uptake is possible, and the tutor's turns show only generic continuation of the prior lesson plan.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn, so no recognition-contingent tutor uptake is possible, and the tutor's turns show only generic continuation of the prior lesson plan.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: No learner turn creates pressure for reversal; the tutor repeats the same instructional move (state the rule, prompt the next column) across all turns without any adaptive change of route, representation, or register.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: No learner turn creates pressure for reversal; the tutor repeats the same instructional move (state the rule, prompt the next column) across all turns without any adaptive change of route, representation, or register.

scored.statedInsightEvidence: Oh, okay: y row = copy the printed y-axis label, then unit only if the axis shows one in brackets.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was on a steady trajectory of incrementally refining the y-row formula by applying the printed-label rule, so the next expected move was simply to fill in the blanks.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: `Sales` stands only if it is the printed y-axis label. `none` stands only if no unit is printed.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: T3 adds conditional validation clauses but does not change route, representation, task type, or register in response to L3's pressure; it repeats the same fill-in-the-blank scaffold with slightly more explicit conditionals.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no tutor turn after the pivot learner turn L4, so no recognition-contingent uptake can be scored.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no tutor turn after the pivot learner turn L4, so no recognition-contingent uptake can be scored.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: `Sales` stands only if it is the printed y-axis label. `none` stands only if no unit is printed.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: T3 adds conditional validation clauses but does not change route, representation, task type, or register in response to L3's pressure; it repeats the same fill-in-the-blank scaffold with slightly more explicit conditionals.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: `Sales` stands only if it is the printed y-axis label. `none` stands only if no unit is printed.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: T3 adds conditional validation clauses but does not change route, representation, task type, or register in response to L3's pressure; it repeats the same fill-in-the-blank scaffold with slightly more explicit conditionals.

scored.statedInsightEvidence: The card over the hunter helps; the arrow starts at the food, point on the one getting energy.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner is expected to continue applying the tail-to-head rule step by step across successive pairs, with the chase-picture interference gradually fading.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: keeps the key strip under the next two labels and holds the card over the hunter picture
scored.tutorReversalJustification: In response to L4's explicit warning that the hunter image will re-trigger the wrong arrow direction, T4 physically occludes the hunter picture while keeping the key strip in place, shifting from verbal rule-repetition to a dual object-work scaffold that changes the perceptual field rather than restating the rule.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn, so recognition-contingent tutor uptake cannot apply; T4 responds to a procedural request rather than to any revised learner framing.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn, so recognition-contingent tutor uptake cannot apply; T4 responds to a procedural request rather than to any revised learner framing.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: keeps the key strip under the next two labels and holds the card over the hunter picture
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: In response to L4's explicit warning that the hunter image will re-trigger the wrong arrow direction, T4 physically occludes the hunter picture while keeping the key strip in place, shifting from verbal rule-repetition to a dual object-work scaffold that changes the perceptual field rather than restating the rule.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: In response to L4's explicit warning that the hunter image will re-trigger the wrong arrow direction, T4 physically occludes the hunter picture while keeping the key strip in place, shifting from verbal rule-repetition to a dual object-work scaffold that changes the perceptual field rather than restating the rule.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner is expected to continue placing each successive force card at its correct contact and confirming the arrow direction, following the tutor's sequential prompts.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor maintains the same sequential card-placement routine throughout with no pressure from the learner triggering a change of route, representation, or register.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn, so no later tutor turn can adapt to a revised learner framing.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn, so no later tutor turn can adapt to a revised learner framing.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor maintains the same sequential card-placement routine throughout with no pressure from the learner triggering a change of route, representation, or register.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor maintains the same sequential card-placement routine throughout with no pressure from the learner triggering a change of route, representation, or register.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner is expected to continue confirming that each contour line carries one fixed elevation number.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: 160 stays on this printed line. 170 sits on the neighboring printed line. Plain check: touch two spots on the 170 line and read its tile.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor introduces a second contour and tile as a modest extension of the same verification routine, but this is an incremental elaboration rather than a route change driven by learner resistance or breakdown.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn, so no recognition-contingent tutor uptake can be scored.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn, so no recognition-contingent tutor uptake can be scored.
